title: The bloated file
prompt: Something in this tree is far bigger than it should be — a classic sign of stashed data. Every file here is under 1 KB except one. du -a . lists the size of every file (in KB); sort -n ranks them; or find . -size +50k goes straight to the point. The flag is buried at the very bottom of the big one (tail is your friend).

buried at the bottom of the biggest file: {{FLAG}}

details.restate: Find the single file that's dramatically larger than every other file in the tree, then read its LAST line — the flag is at the bottom, past hundreds of padding lines.

io.because: du -a sizes every file, sort -n ranks numerically, tail shows the top of the ranking
find can filter directly on size when you know the threshold

help.plain: Rank everything by size, grab the outlier, read its end — not its beginning.
help.analogy: Weighing the luggage to find the smuggler — you don't open every bag, the scale points at one.
help.mnemonic: du -a | sort -n: measure all, rank, read the bottom of the list.
help.nudge: Two tools can rank or filter by size. And once you have the file: 700 lines of noise, flag at the end — which reading command starts from the end?
